comment.body: I can just share my observations on patterns - dealer short fly ( short ATM, long OTM ) - which indicates market is long ATM and selling OTM vol. overall positive speed and short color .

comment.body: Short speed below and long above , with stepper to the upside , indicting market isn’t completely confident about left tail , but strongly expects realized vol to lower / compress as move towards 6900.
comment.raw_body: Short speed below and long above , with stepper to the upside , indicting market isn’t completely confident about left tail , but strongly expects realized vol to lower / compress as move towards 6900.

comment.body:
Still waking up but I looked at it a bit last night (with the annotated forecast from https://open.substack.com/chat/posts/2aab643b-d095-4c00-8b42-4f35379fb002
 as reference) ... right now ES puts SPX about 6830, which is still in the (very wide) negative speed territory, and is trending slightly downwards.

Given this is FOMC day, 6850 is a likely upper limit since at least implied volatility will spike going into it.  Conversely, participants do not expect the market to go below 6810-20, but there is not much conviction in that.

On the downside, MM's have a large number of calls around 6690 with high color, which will provide an increasingly solid wall throughout the day.

After noon if the market is above 6780, additional support will appear - but below 6770, it will become increasingly difficult to break above it, with that resistance band dropping to 6750 near EOD.

comment.body: BTW, if you have an OD account and plot yesterday's PA (sic) the OD chart actually explains the lack-of-action yesterday, it went into a negative speed territory around 11, vol was too high to even touch the yellow line, and it drifted down along the green line until power hour

comment.body: BTW the difficulty with ES bookmap is unless you have *very expensive* data you only get the nearest 5 prices
comment.raw_body: BTW the difficulty with ES bookmap is unless you have *very expensive* data you only get the nearest 5 prices

comment.body: But looking at bookmap synergizes with the OD outlook, there is considerable selling pressure above the current price.  I think support will appear to prevent the market from going *too* far down at least before the FOMC announcement, but I don't see an upside this morning
